title: My Journey
description: Honestly, when I first started coding, I had no clue what I was doing. Everything looked like random symbols on a screen. I spent way too many hours on YouTube tutorials just to understand the basics. Then one day I discovered Tkinter in Python — and that changed everything. Suddenly I could actually build stuff with a real interface. My first real project? A food ordering app inspired by the menu system at my school cafeteria. Nothing fancy, but I was hooked. From there I just kept going, picked up new languages, started bigger projects, and never really stopped.
subTitle: Right now I'm deep into Linux server management and building APIs.
subDescription: Now, let me take you through some of my projects...

title: My Experience
description: I've been writing code for about 3 years now. Started with Python, then moved into JavaScript and TypeScript for both frontend and backend stuff. I spend most of my time building APIs and writing object-oriented code. I'm still learning web dev every day, but I can already put together full pages in Angular and get them running on an AWS VM. I also picked up some C++ and Java basics in school — nothing crazy, but enough to understand how things work under the hood. I just like figuring things out and getting better at what I do, one project at a time.
subTitle: Writing code since I was 13.

title: Skills I have Learned
description: Python was my starting point — OOP, Tkinter, Pycord, SQLite, all that good stuff. After about a year I jumped into TypeScript and Angular, which is what I mainly use for web projects now. I also run Linux (RaspiOS) on a VM where I host my Discord bot and manage everything with Crontab. Lately I've been getting into API development with ExpressJS, which has been fun to learn.
subTitle: Always picking up something new — tools, frameworks, whatever catches my interest.
